content.full_text:
TELEPHONE ADVICE
T-83-282

Attorney: Alice Hughes

Date: 30 November 1983

Advice Letter: _________________________________________________________________

Person Requesting Advice

Senator Boatwright's Aide

_____________________________________________________________________________

_____________________________________________________________________________

_____________________________________________________________________________

Telephone: _________________________________________________________________

Question: Does a filer have to report gifts received from
. a friend whom he is dating?




Advice: I advised him that gifts of equal value exchanged
between friends on holidays, birthdays, or similar
occasions, other than from a lobbyist, are exempt
from disclosure. (See FPPC Reg. 18727(b)).

He requested that I check "old" 721 manuals to see how
long that advice had been listed as an exception. I
called him back to advise him that the exemption has
been listed since 1977.

In addition, I advised him of the memo from Ted Prim
dated December 15, 1979, indicating that gifts exchanged
between friends who have a bona fide dating relationship
are not reportable (see attached.)

Advice: I advised him that gifts of equal value exchanged

He requested that I check "old" 721 manuals to see how
long that advice had been listed as an exception. I
called him back to advise him that the exemption has
been listed since 1977.

In addition, I advised him of the memo from Ted Prim
dated December 15, 1979, indicating that gifts exchanged
between friends who have a bona fide dating relationship
are not reportable (see attached.)

sections.conclusion: I advised him that gifts of equal value exchanged between friends on holidays, birthdays, or similar occasions, other than from a lobbyist, are exempt from disclosure. (See FPPC Reg. 18727(b)). He requested that I check "old" 721 manuals to see how long that advice had been listed as an exception. I called him back to advise him that the exemption has been listed since 1977. In addition, I advised him of the memo from Ted Prim dated December 15, 1979, indicating that gifts exchanged between friends who have a bona fide dating relationship are not reportable (see attached.)
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked whether gifts received from a dating partner must be reported as gifts under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advised that gifts of equal value exchanged between friends on holidays, birthdays, or similar occasions are exempt from disclosure under Regulation 18727(b), and that this exemption applies to gifts exchanged between friends with a bona fide dating relationship. The exemption has been in place since 1977 and is further supported by a December 15, 1979 memo from Ted Prim.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Telephone advice format with duplicated sections in source document. Advice references external memo and regulation. Document appears to be from November 50, 1983 (likely November 30, 1983 based on context).

embedding.summary: FPPC telephone advice letter addressing whether gifts received from a dating partner must be reported. FPPC advised that such gifts are exempt from disclosure when exchanged between friends on holidays, birthdays, or similar occasions, provided the gift giver is not a lobbyist.
